item.text:
**Reviewing totalKills removal risk**

The increment of totalKills was removed for nuked enemies while trackKill remains, which may cause undercounting if totalKills feeds stats or progression. This is medium-risk but possibly intentional since trackKill existed before and increments separately; evidence on usage is lacking. Also, disabling depthTest on boss projectiles could affect rendering but seems purposeful.

**Flagging instanced mesh leak and totalKills undercount**

The new removal logic only disposes boss projectile meshes when _instIdx exists, risking memory leaks for any non-instanced meshes still in use—this is a high-severity regression needing fallback cleanup. Also, activateNuke no longer increments totalKills for nuked enemies, risking undercounting stats or progression if totalKills is used, a medium severity issue.

{"headline":"Nightly Review: 2 actionable findings","overall_risk":"medium","verdict":"issues found","summary":"Two changes introduce risk: boss projectile cleanup now skips non-instanced meshes (potential leak/artifacts), and nuked enemies no longer increment `game.totalKills` (possible stat/progression regression).","focus_areas":["boss projectile cleanup","kill counters/progression"],"findings":[{"title":"Boss projectiles may leak if `_instIdx` is missing","category":"stability","severity":"high","confidence":0.63,"file":"main.js","start_line":8127,"end_line":9903,"impact":"If any boss projectile is still backed by a unique mesh (or `_instIdx` isn’t set for some spawn path), the new removal path only calls `releaseBossProjIndex` and skips `scene.remove`/`disposeObject3D`. The projectile is spliced from the array but its mesh remains in the scene, causing invisible collision risks or visible artifacts and a memory leak that can degrade VR performance over time.","evidence":"All three removal sites now do `if (proj._instIdx !== undefined) releaseBossProjIndex(...)` and then splice, with no fallback removal/dispose when `_instIdx` is undefined (previously always removed/disposed).","recommendation":"Add a fallback: if `_instIdx` is undefined, remove and dispose the mesh as before. Alternatively, guarantee `_instIdx` is always set for every boss projectile creation path."},{"title":"Nuke kills no longer increment `game.totalKills`","category":"correctness","severity":"medium","confidence":0.55,"file":"main.js","start_line":3352,"end_line":3376,"impact":"Total-kill-based stats, unlocks, or end-of-level reporting may undercount nuked enemies, creating progression or UI inconsistencies.","evidence":"`game.totalKills++` was removed from the nuke loop while `game.kills++` and `trackKill(false)` remain.","recommendation":"Either restore `game.totalKills++` for nuked enemies or confirm `trackKill(false)` updates `totalKills` and remove other redundant increments for consistency."}]}
